start.text: The sun is rising over the ThunderClan camp. You are Firepaw, a new apprentice. Your ginger fur glows in the light. Your mentor, Lionheart, approaches you. 'Today, Firepaw, you will go on your first border patrol. We must check the scent markers near the ShadowClan border. Are you ready?'

choices.text: Ask what a border patrol does

explain_patrol.text: Lionheart smiles. 'A patrol checks our borders to make sure other Clans are not stealing our prey or entering our territory. We also leave fresh scent markers. It is the duty of every warrior.'

leave_camp.text: You, Lionheart, and a grey warrior named Graypaw leave the camp. The forest is full of smells. You reach the Sunningrocks. These rocks are often fought over by ThunderClan and RiverClan. Suddenly, you hear a rustle in the ferns.

investigate_alone.text: You creep toward the ferns, trying to be silent. But you trip over a fallen branch! *Snap!* A large RiverClan warrior jumps out and pins you down! 'What is a ThunderClan apprentice doing here?' he growls.

choices.text: Wait for Lionheart to rescue you

lionheart_rescue.text: Lionheart leaps from the shadows and knocks the RiverClan cat away. 'Go back to your own territory!' he bellows. The RiverClan cat flees. Lionheart looks at you sternly. 'Firepaw, you must never investigate alone on a patrol. It was a mistake.'

choices.text: I'm sorry, I've learned my lesson

tell_lionheart.text: Lionheart signals for you to stay back. He creeps forward and finds... a small mouse! 'Good ears, Firepaw,' he says. 'But we are here for the border, not for hunting. Let's continue toward the ShadowClan border.'

shadowclan_border.text: The forest becomes darker and the air smells like pine needles. This is the edge of ShadowClan territory. You see a fresh scent marker, but it doesn't smell like ShadowClan. It smells like a rogue cat!

choices.text: Ignore it and keep marking the border

ignore_rogue.text: You continue marking the border, but Lionheart stops you. 'Firepaw, do you not smell that? A rogue is near our territory. We cannot ignore a threat to the Clan.' You realize you should have paid more attention.

follow_rogue.text: The scent leads you to a deep ravine. At the bottom, you see a ragged-looking tomcat eating a piece of prey—a squirrel stolen from ThunderClan territory! Lionheart whispers, 'We must surround him.'

choices.text: Go left to cut off his escape
Go right to trap him against the rocks

charge_middle.text: You let out a battle cry and dash down the slope! The rogue is startled, but he is an experienced fighter. He swats you aside and disappears into the thick undergrowth. Lionheart sighs. 'Patience is a warrior's virtue, Firepaw. Now we have lost him.'

choices.text: Let's try to find his trail again

go_left.text: You move silently to the left. Graypaw goes right, and Lionheart stays in the middle. The rogue sees Lionheart and tries to run your way! You stand your ground.

go_right.text: You move to the right, but the ground is slippery with wet moss. You slide down the bank with a loud yowl! The rogue hears you and vanishes before the patrol can close in. 'Be more careful where you step,' Lionheart warns.

choices.text: Climb back up and look for the scent

talk_rogue.text: You try to speak, but the rogue isn't interested in words. He hisses and scratches your nose! He manages to scramble past you and escapes. Lionheart says, 'Rogues do not follow the Warrior Code. You cannot reason with a thief.'

choices.text: I won't let him get away next time

fight_rogue.text: You swipe at the rogue's face as he tries to pass. He is surprised by your speed! Lionheart and Graypaw close in. The rogue realizes he is trapped. 'Fine!' he snarls. 'Take your scrawny squirrel. I was leaving anyway.'

choices.text: Make sure he leaves the territory

chase_out.text: The three of you escort the rogue back to the Twolegplace. He won't be coming back soon. On the way back to camp, Lionheart looks pleased. 'You showed great courage, Firepaw. But remember, a warrior's strength comes from his Clan, not just himself.'

ask_starclan.text: 'Do you think StarClan was watching us?' you ask. Lionheart looks up at the sky, where the first stars are appearing. 'StarClan is always watching, Firepaw. They guide our paws when the path is dark. They would be proud of you today.'

return_camp.text: You return to the ThunderClan camp as heroes. Bluestar, the leader, is waiting. Lionheart tells her of your bravery and your mistakes. Bluestar nods. 'Mistakes are the best teachers, Firepaw. You have done well for your first patrol.'

choices.text: Share your prey with the elders

ending.text: You take a fresh-kill mouse to the elders' den. They tell you stories of great battles from the past. You feel like a true part of ThunderClan now. You fall asleep in the apprentices' den, dreaming of the silver stars of StarClan.

vocabulary.apprentice: a young cat training to become a fighter
vocabulary.patrol: a group of cats that protects the forest area
vocabulary.territory: the area of land that belongs to a specific group
vocabulary.scent markers: smells left by cats to show where their borders are
vocabulary.rogue: a cat who lives alone and does not follow the rules
vocabulary.ravine: a deep, narrow valley with steep sides
vocabulary.patience: the ability to wait without getting angry or upset
vocabulary.virtue: a good quality or way of behaving
vocabulary.escort: to go with someone to make sure they get to a place safely or leave
vocabulary.Twolegplace: the area where humans live in houses
vocabulary.ancestors: family members from a long time ago
vocabulary.prophetic: predicting what will happen in the future
vocabulary.feral: living in the wild; not tame
vocabulary.hierarchy: a system where members are ranked according to status
vocabulary.destiny: what is supposed to happen in the future
vocabulary.courage: bravery; being able to do something even if you are afraid
vocabulary.sternly: in a serious and strict way
vocabulary.rustle: a soft, crackling sound made by dry leaves or plants
vocabulary.undergrowth: small trees and bushes growing under taller trees
